page: Amazon Web Services c6a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs derived from 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and relies on the Nitro hypervisor. Network connectivity provides a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25% of evaluated systems. Multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests, falls within the middle 50% average. Memory read bandwidth is average, reaching 1140.64 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 39.28 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, uncached large block memory write bandwidth is a relative bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% at 21.76 GB/sec. Cryptographic and integer operations are supported by hardware-accelerated instructions such as AES, VAES, and SHA-NI, though the processor lacks AVX-512 instruction support.

The c6a.4xlarge offers a compute-dense profile utilizing AMD EPYC architecture, which provides a cost-efficient alternative to comparable memory-heavy or GPU-accelerated instances for compute-bound tasks. Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and average database and web serving benchmarks, this instance is appropriate for workloads such as static web serving, single-threaded or moderately multi-threaded data compression, and small-scale language model text generation. It is less suitable for memory-write intensive applications or workloads requiring local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c6a.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a 3.6 GHz clock speed. It is configured with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution and efficient small-model text generation, though uncached large-block memory write bandwidth is relatively weak. Lacking AVX-512 support, it relies on AES, VAES, and SHA-NI for cryptographic tasks. This instance is suited for compute-bound workloads, static web serving, and moderate data processing tasks that do not require high memory-to-CPU ratios or local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 6 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for compute-bound workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and balanced memory bandwidth for compute-intensive workloads.

32.0 GB of DDR4 system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core.
Strong single-core CPU performance and small-model text generation capabilities.
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth utilizing the Nitro hypervisor.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile.
Appropriate for static web serving, data compression, and compute-bound tasks.
